title: Anti-Forensics / Log Wipe (Linux)
briefing: $USER1 logged into $TARGET from $SRC1, then the shell history was destroyed and a system log file was truncated/deleted. The attacker is covering their tracks. Recognise the anti-forensics, and understand what it means for the rest of your investigation.
dashboard_hint: agent.name: $TARGET. rule.id 5715 (login) then 100213 (history tampering) and 100214 (/var/log/... tampering). The very fact these events reached the SIEM is because Wazuh forwards logs OFF the host in real time — the attacker wiped the LOCAL copy but not the forwarded one. Do not trust the host's on-disk logs from here.

questions.prompt: What is the attacker's intent here?
options: Anti-forensics — destroying evidence of their activity
questions.answer: Anti-forensics — destroying evidence of their activity
questions.explain: Wiping shell history and truncating system logs is deliberate evidence destruction (defense evasion).
All of the activity lands on $TARGET; the other hosts are unaffected.
History tampering (100213) and /var/log tampering (100214) are the anti-forensic actions; the login itself is not.

questions.prompt: What does the log wipe mean for your investigation?
options: The host's local logs are now untrustworthy — pivot to the forwarded SIEM/central copy
The investigation is over; there is no evidence left
Nothing — local logs are always complete
questions.answer: The host's local logs are now untrustworthy — pivot to the forwarded SIEM/central copy
questions.explain: You only see these events because Wazuh already forwarded them. Treat on-host logs as tampered and rely on the central copy.
All the tampering commands ran under $USER1's session.

options: T1070.002 Clear Linux or Mac System Logs
answer: T1070.002 Clear Linux or Mac System Logs
questions.explain: History wipe (T1070.003), system-log wipe (T1070.002), and the valid-account login (T1078). Kerberoasting is Windows/AD and absent.

options: Preserve/collect the forwarded logs before they age out
Isolate $TARGET and image it for forensics
Rotate $USER1 and hunt for what the history wipe was hiding
Assume the host is fully compromised
Close the alert — the logs are gone so there's nothing to do
answer: Preserve/collect the forwarded logs before they age out
Isolate $TARGET and image it for forensics
Rotate $USER1 and hunt for what the history wipe was hiding
Assume the host is fully compromised
questions.explain: Anti-forensics raises confidence of compromise: preserve the off-host evidence, isolate/image, rotate creds, and hunt for the activity the wipe was meant to hide.
